name: Liquid Sunshine Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 butternut squash (or 1 can of pumpkin puree)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Cut the squash in half, remove seeds and stringy membrane. Place cut side down on a baking sheet. Pierce the skin with a fork a few times. Cook for 40 minutes or until soft.
3. Chop onions and sauté in a soup pot until caramelized. Add broth as needed. If using mushrooms, add them to the pot when onions are done and cook until liquid is released and cooked down.
4. Rinse lentils. For a quicker soup, soak them in the broth.
5. Scoop out the squash flesh and puree it with orange juice.
6. Once onions are caramelized (and mushrooms are done), add all ingredients to the onion cooking pot. Simmer gently until lentils are cooked. If using fresh squash, you may wish to add a small amount of onion juice to balance the sweetness. If using canned pumpkin, consider adding a bit of sugar to enhance the sweetness.
